title: 1301. Which organization is responsible for the research and development of defence technologies in India?
hint: It was established in 1958.
summary: DRDO is India's premier defence research and development organization.

title: 1302. Which is the supreme command authority of the Indian Armed Forces?
hint: The position is held by the constitutional head of India.
summary: The President of India is the Supreme Commander of the Armed Forces.

title: 1303. Which is the oldest armed force of India?
hint: Its origins date back to the East India Company.
summary: The Indian Army is the oldest of India's armed forces.

title: 1304. The headquarters of the Indian Navy is located in:
hint: It is India's financial capital.
summary: The Naval Headquarters is in New Delhi, while The Indian Western Naval Command in Mumbai.

title: 1305. Which force is primarily responsible for guarding India's international land borders?
hint: It is one of India's Central Armed Police Forces.
summary: The Border Security Force guards most of India's international land borders.

title: 1306. Which Indian defence force celebrates its Raising Day on 15 January?
hint: Field Marshal K.M. Cariappa became its first Indian Commander-in-Chief on this date.
summary: Indian Army Day is celebrated every year on 15 January.

title: 1307. Which missile is India's first indigenously developed ballistic missile?
hint: It is named after a mythological weapon.
summary: Prithvi was India's first indigenously developed ballistic missile.

title: 1308. Which missile is jointly developed by India and Russia?
hint: Its name is derived from two rivers.
summary: BrahMos is a joint venture between India and Russia.

title: 1309. Which organization manufactures the Tejas Light Combat Aircraft?
hint: It is a public sector aerospace company.
summary: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited manufactures the Tejas fighter aircraft.

title: 1310. Which Indian armed force is responsible for safeguarding India's maritime interests?
hint: Its motto is 'Sham No Varunah'.
summary: The Indian Navy protects India's maritime interests and sea lanes.

title: 1311. Which was the first indigenously built aircraft carrier of India?
hint: It was commissioned in 2022.
summary: INS Vikrant is India's first indigenously built aircraft carrier.

title: 1312. Which missile is India's long-range surface-to-air missile developed jointly with Israel?
hint: It is also deployed by the Indian Navy.
summary: Barak-8 is jointly developed by India and Israel.

title: 1313. The headquarters of the Integrated Defence Staff (IDS) is located in:
hint: It is the national capital.
summary: The Integrated Defence Staff is headquartered in New Delhi.

title: 1314. Which Indian Air Force exercise is conducted jointly with the Royal Air Force (UK)?
hint: Its name means 'to fly together'.
summary: Exercise Indradhanush is conducted between India and the United Kingdom.

title: 1315. Which missile is designed primarily as an anti-tank guided missile (ATGM)?
hint: It is named after a snake.
summary: Nag is India's indigenous third-generation anti-tank guided missile.

title: 1316. Which organization is responsible for manufacturing India's Arjun Main Battle Tank?
hint: It is headquartered in Avadi, Chennai.
summary: Heavy Vehicles Factory (HVF), Avadi manufactures the Arjun MBT.

title: 1317. Which Indian missile is capable of carrying both conventional and nuclear warheads?
hint: It is a family of ballistic missiles.
summary: The Agni missile series is capable of carrying conventional and nuclear warheads.

title: 1318. Which defence PSU is India's leading warship builder on the west coast?
hint: It is based in Mumbai.
summary: Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited builds destroyers, frigates and submarines.

title: 1319. Which Indian defence exercise is conducted jointly with the United States Army?
hint: It is an annual bilateral army exercise.
summary: Exercise Yudh Abhyas is conducted between the Indian Army and the U.S. Army.

title: 1320. The motto 'Touch the Sky with Glory' is associated with which branch of the Indian Armed Forces?
hint: It operates fighter aircraft and helicopters.
summary: The motto 'Touch the Sky with Glory' belongs to the Indian Air Force.

title: 1321. Which missile is India's first indigenous beyond-visual-range air-to-air missile (BVRAAM)?
hint: It has been integrated with the Tejas fighter aircraft.
summary: Astra is India's first indigenously developed beyond-visual-range air-to-air missile.

title: 1322. Which defence PSU manufactures the Tejas Light Combat Aircraft?
hint: It is headquartered in Bengaluru.
summary: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) manufactures the Tejas Light Combat Aircraft.

title: 1323. Which Indian missile system is a surface-to-air missile designed for air defence?
hint: It is named after the sky.
summary: Akash is India's indigenously developed medium-range surface-to-air missile system.

title: 1324. Exercise 'Malabar' is a multilateral naval exercise involving India and which of the following countries?
hint: The Quad nations participate in it.
summary: Exercise Malabar is conducted by India, the United States, Japan and Australia.

title: 1325. Which organization manufactures the Pinaka Multi-Barrel Rocket Launcher (MBRL)?
hint: It is produced by defence PSUs along with private industry.
summary: Pinaka is manufactured by Bharat Earth Movers Limited (BEML), Tata, Larsen & Toubro and others, while its rockets are produced by Bharat Dynamics Limited and other partners.

title: 1326. Which Indian missile is a submarine-launched ballistic missile (SLBM) with a range of about 750 km?
hint: It is also known as B-05.
summary: K-15 (Sagarika) is India's first operational submarine-launched ballistic missile.

title: 1327. Which organization develops India's indigenous ballistic missile defence (BMD) programme?
hint: It is India's premier defence R&D agency.
summary: The Ballistic Missile Defence programme is developed by the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO).

title: 1328. Exercise 'Varuna' is a bilateral naval exercise between India and:
hint: It is conducted with a European country.
summary: Exercise Varuna is the bilateral naval exercise conducted between the Indian Navy and the French Navy.

title: 1329. Which organization is responsible for producing the BrahMos missile?
hint: It is an Indo-Russian joint venture.
summary: BrahMos Aerospace is the Indo-Russian joint venture responsible for manufacturing the BrahMos missile.

title: 1330. Which Indian Navy aircraft is primarily used for long-range maritime reconnaissance and anti-submarine warfare?
hint: It is based on the Boeing 737 platform.
summary: The P-8I Poseidon is India's primary long-range maritime reconnaissance and anti-submarine warfare aircraft.

title: 1331. Which Indian missile is an indigenously developed anti-radiation missile designed to destroy enemy radar systems?
hint: Its name is derived from the Sanskrit word for 'rudder' or 'guidance'.
summary: Rudram-1 is India's first indigenous air-launched anti-radiation missile developed by DRDO.

title: 1332. The National Defence Academy (NDA) is located at:
hint: It is situated near Pune.
summary: The National Defence Academy is located at Khadakwasla, Maharashtra.

title: 1333. Which is the premier training academy for officers of the Indian Air Force?
hint: It is located in Hyderabad.
summary: The Air Force Academy at Dundigal trains officers of the Indian Air Force.

title: 1334. Which Indian naval command is headquartered at Visakhapatnam?
hint: It is responsible for operations in the Bay of Bengal.
summary: The Eastern Naval Command is headquartered at Visakhapatnam.

title: 1335. Which Indian defence PSU specializes primarily in the production of guided missiles and underwater weapons?
hint: Its headquarters is in Hyderabad.
summary: Bharat Dynamics Limited (BDL) manufactures guided missiles, torpedoes and related defence equipment.

title: 1336. Which is India's only tri-service command?
hint: It is headquartered at Port Blair.
summary: The Andaman and Nicobar Command is India's first and only tri-service command.

title: 1337. Which missile is an indigenously developed man-portable anti-tank guided missile (ATGM) by DRDO?
hint: It is the MPATGM project.
summary: MPATGM is an indigenous man-portable anti-tank guided missile developed by DRDO.

title: 1338. The Indian Military Academy (IMA) is located at:
hint: It is in Uttarakhand.
summary: The Indian Military Academy is located in Dehradun.

title: 1339. Which command of the Indian Navy is headquartered at Kochi?
hint: It is primarily the Navy's training command.
summary: The Southern Naval Command is headquartered at Kochi.

title: 1340. Which Indian defence PSU is headquartered in Bengaluru and specializes in military electronics, radars and communication systems?
hint: It is commonly abbreviated as BEL.
summary: Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) is India's leading defence electronics PSU.

title: 1341. Which Indian Air Force command is responsible for air operations in the western region of India?
hint: Its headquarters is located in New Delhi.
summary: Western Air Command is one of the major operational commands of the Indian Air Force.

title: 1342. Which Indian naval ship is the first indigenous aircraft carrier built by Cochin Shipyard Limited?
hint: It was commissioned into the Indian Navy in 2022.
summary: INS Vikrant (IAC-1) is India's first indigenous aircraft carrier.

title: 1343. Who is the Supreme Commander of the Indian Armed Forces according to the Constitution of India?
hint: It is the highest constitutional office in India.
summary: The President of India is the Supreme Commander of the Indian Armed Forces.

title: 1344. Which operation was launched by the Indian Armed Forces during the Kargil War in 1999?
hint: It was a military operation to evict Pakistani forces from Kargil heights.
summary: Operation Vijay was launched during the Kargil conflict of 1999.

title: 1345. Which Indian missile is a supersonic cruise missile jointly developed with Russia?
hint: Its name combines two rivers.
summary: BrahMos is a supersonic cruise missile developed through India-Russia cooperation.

title: 1346. Which Indian Army command is responsible for the defence of the northern borders including Ladakh region?
hint: Its headquarters is located in Udhampur.
summary: Northern Command of the Indian Army is headquartered at Udhampur, Jammu and Kashmir.

title: 1347. Which Indian defence research laboratory is associated with missile development?
hint: It is located in Hyderabad.
summary: Research Centre Imarat (RCI) is a DRDO laboratory involved in missile systems development.

title: 1348. Which operation of the Indian Army is associated with securing the Siachen Glacier?
hint: It was launched in 1984.
summary: Operation Meghdoot was launched in 1984 to secure the Siachen Glacier.

title: 1349. Which Indian naval command is headquartered in Mumbai?
hint: It is responsible for India's western maritime region.
summary: Western Naval Command is headquartered in Mumbai.

title: 1350. Which Indian fighter aircraft is known as the Light Combat Aircraft (LCA)?
hint: It was developed by HAL with support from DRDO agencies.
summary: Tejas is India's indigenous Light Combat Aircraft.
